speaker: TROILUS
text: Call here my varlet; I’ll unarm again.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Why should I war without the walls of Troy

speaker: TROILUS
text: That find such cruel battle here within?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Each Trojan that is master of his heart,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let him to field; Troilus, alas, hath none.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Will this gear ne’er be mended?

speaker: TROILUS
text: The Greeks are strong and skilful to their strength,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Fierce to their skill, and to their fierceness valiant;

speaker: TROILUS
text: But I am weaker than a woman’s tear,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Tamer than sleep, fonder than ignorance,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Less valiant than the virgin in the night,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And skilless as unpracticed infancy.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Well, I have told you enough of this. For my

speaker: PANDARUS
text: part, I’ll not meddle nor make no farther. He that will

speaker: PANDARUS
text: have a cake out of the wheat must tarry the grinding.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Have I not tarried?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Ay, the grinding; but you must tarry the

speaker: PANDARUS
text: bolting.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Have I not tarried?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Ay, the bolting; but you must tarry the

speaker: PANDARUS
text: leavening.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Still have I tarried.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Ay, to the leavening; but here’s yet in the word

speaker: PANDARUS
text: hereafter the kneading, the making of the cake, the

speaker: PANDARUS
text: heating the oven, and the baking. Nay, you must stay

speaker: PANDARUS
text: the cooling too, or you may chance burn your lips.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Patience herself, what goddess e’er she be,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Doth lesser blench at suff’rance than I do.

speaker: TROILUS
text: At Priam’s royal table do I sit

speaker: TROILUS
text: And when fair Cressid comes into my thoughts—

speaker: TROILUS
text: So, traitor! “When she comes”? When is she thence?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Well, she looked yesternight fairer than ever

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I saw her look, or any woman else.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I was about to tell thee: when my heart,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As wedgèd with a sigh, would rive in twain,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Lest Hector or my father should perceive me,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I have, as when the sun doth light a-scorn,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Buried this sigh in wrinkle of a smile;

speaker: TROILUS
text: But sorrow that is couched in seeming gladness

speaker: TROILUS
text: Is like that mirth fate turns to sudden sadness.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: An her hair were not somewhat darker than

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Helen’s—well, go to—there were no more comparison

speaker: PANDARUS
text: between the women. But, for my part, she is

speaker: PANDARUS
text: my kinswoman; I would not, as they term it, praise

speaker: PANDARUS
text: her, but I would somebody had heard her talk yesterday,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: as I did. I will not dispraise your sister Cassandra’s

speaker: PANDARUS
text: wit, but—

speaker: TROILUS
text: O, Pandarus! I tell thee, Pandarus:

speaker: TROILUS
text: When I do tell thee there my hopes lie drowned,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Reply not in how many fathoms deep

speaker: TROILUS
text: They lie indrenched. I tell thee I am mad

speaker: TROILUS
text: In Cressid’s love. Thou answer’st she is fair;

speaker: TROILUS
text: Pourest in the open ulcer of my heart

speaker: TROILUS
text: Her eyes, her hair, her cheek, her gait, her voice;

speaker: TROILUS
text: Handiest in thy discourse—O—that her hand,

speaker: TROILUS
text: In whose comparison all whites are ink

speaker: TROILUS
text: Writing their own reproach, to whose soft seizure

speaker: TROILUS
text: The cygnet’s down is harsh, and spirit of sense

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hard as the palm of plowman. This thou tell’st me,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As true thou tell’st me, when I say I love her.

speaker: TROILUS
text: But, saying thus, instead of oil and balm

speaker: TROILUS
text: Thou lay’st in every gash that love hath given me

speaker: TROILUS
text: The knife that made it.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I speak no more than truth.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Thou dost not speak so much.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Faith, I’ll not meddle in it. Let her be as she

speaker: PANDARUS
text: is. If she be fair, ’tis the better for her; an she be

speaker: PANDARUS
text: not, she has the mends in her own hands.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Good Pandarus—how now, Pandarus?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I have had my labor for my travail, ill thought

speaker: PANDARUS
text: on of her, and ill thought on of you; gone between

speaker: PANDARUS
text: and between, but small thanks for my labor.

speaker: TROILUS
text: What, art thou angry, Pandarus? What, with

speaker: TROILUS
text: me?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Because she’s kin to me, therefore she’s not

speaker: PANDARUS
text: so fair as Helen; an she were not kin to me, she

speaker: PANDARUS
text: would be as fair o’ Friday as Helen is on Sunday.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: But what care I? I care not an she were a blackamoor;

speaker: PANDARUS
text: ’tis all one to me.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Say I she is not fair?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I do not care whether you do or no. She’s a

speaker: PANDARUS
text: fool to stay behind her father. Let her to the Greeks,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: and so I’ll tell her the next time I see her. For my

speaker: PANDARUS
text: part, I’ll meddle nor make no more i’ th’ matter.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Pandarus—

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Not I.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Sweet Pandarus—

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Pray you speak no more to me. I will leave

speaker: PANDARUS
text: all as I found it, and there an end.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Peace, you ungracious clamors! Peace, rude sounds!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Fools on both sides! Helen must needs be fair

speaker: TROILUS
text: When with your blood you daily paint her thus.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I cannot fight upon this argument;

speaker: TROILUS
text: It is too starved a subject for my sword.

speaker: TROILUS
text: But Pandarus—O gods, how do you plague me!

speaker: TROILUS
text: I cannot come to Cressid but by Pandar,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And he’s as tetchy to be wooed to woo

speaker: TROILUS
text: As she is stubborn-chaste against all suit.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Tell me, Apollo, for thy Daphnes love,

speaker: TROILUS
text: What Cressid is, what Pandar, and what we.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Her bed is India; there she lies, a pearl.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Between our Ilium and where she resides,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let it be called the wild and wand’ring flood,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Ourself the merchant, and this sailing Pandar

speaker: TROILUS
text: Our doubtful hope, our convoy, and our bark.

speaker: AENEAS
text: How now, Prince Troilus? Wherefore not afield?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Because not there. This woman’s answer sorts,

speaker: TROILUS
text: For womanish it is to be from thence.

speaker: TROILUS
text: What news, Aeneas, from the field today?

speaker: AENEAS
text: That Paris is returnèd home, and hurt.

speaker: TROILUS
text: By whom, Aeneas?

speaker: AENEAS
text: Troilus, by Menelaus.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let Paris bleed. ’Tis but a scar to scorn;

speaker: TROILUS
text: Paris is gored with Menelaus’ horn.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Hark what good sport is out of town today!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Better at home, if “would I might” were “may.”

speaker: TROILUS
text: But to the sport abroad. Are you bound thither?

speaker: AENEAS
text: In all swift haste.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Come, go we then together.
